title: Add 'Apply to be a Moderator' section to Settings

requirements.summary: Add an 'Apply to be a Moderator' section to SettingsPage.tsx with a 1000-character textarea, live character counter, and submit button, styled consistently with the Sonic visual theme
acceptanceCriteria: An 'Apply to be a Moderator' section is visible in SettingsPage.tsx for authenticated users.
The section is visually distinct from the appeals and ideas sections but follows the same card/panel styling consistent with the Sonic visual theme.
The textarea accepts up to 1000 characters and displays a live character counter (e.g. '450 / 1000').
The submit button is clearly labelled (e.g. 'Submit Application').
The section is styled consistently with the existing Sonic visual theme (cobalt blue/yellow palette, Fredoka One or Nunito font, rounded styling).

file_operations.description: Add a new 'Apply to be a Moderator' section below the existing ideas section. Include a textarea element with maxLength={1000} that accepts plain text only. Add a character counter display showing remaining characters (e.g., '450 / 1000'). Include a submit button labeled 'Submit Application'. Style the section using the sonic-card class and sonic-yellow accent color to match the existing appeals and ideas sections while remaining visually distinct. Use state to track the textarea value and disable the submit button when the textarea is empty or contains only whitespace.

requirements.summary: When the moderator application form is submitted, open the moderator review URL in a new tab with the application message and user identity appended, then display an in-page confirmation message and clear the form
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking submit opens https://scrambly-moderator-lq2.caffeine.xyz/#caffeineAdminToken=d56558c15a1ee884ff360f38b77d9e18b6876ade2990f07ccf750d3a64b064b4 in a new browser tab.
The submission does not block or navigate away from the current settings page.
An in-page confirmation message is displayed after the form is submitted.
The form textarea is cleared after a successful submission.
Submitting an empty or whitespace-only textarea is prevented — the button is disabled or an inline validation error is shown if the field is empty.

file_operations.description: Implement the submit handler for the moderator application form. On submit, construct a URL by appending the application message and the user's principal ID as hash parameters to https://scrambly-moderator-lq2.caffeine.xyz/#caffeineAdminToken=d56558c15a1ee884ff360f38b77d9e18b6876ade2990f07ccf750d3a64b064b4 (e.g., add &message=<encoded_message>&user=<principal_string>). Use window.open() with '_blank' to open the URL in a new tab. After opening the URL, display a confirmation message using the toast notification system (e.g., 'Your application has been submitted! A moderator will review it soon.'). Clear the textarea by resetting the form state. Ensure the submit button is disabled when the textarea is empty or contains only whitespace. Access the user's principal from the identity context using useInternetIdentity().identity.getPrincipal().toString().
